probes.name: basic_adventure_request
probes.prompt: Opowiadanie na dobranoc, please create a new adventure for me. I want something immersive with interesting characters. Give me the full setup: title, setting, the characters I can meet, and the opening narration to get us started.
probes.criteria:
The adventure_generator agent must produce a complete adventure package: a title, a defined setting and genre with tone, 3-5 characters (exactly one player avatar) each with a secret, seeded world aspects, and an opening narration delivered as a short punchy intro. The response should be immersive and ready to play.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 5 ('So can you tell me some story?') and 7 ('Can you tell me another story?') — direct requests for story/adventure content.

probes.name: non_pony_work_scenario
probes.prompt: I'd like to try a roleplay adventure that's NOT fantasy or pony-themed. Can you set up a workplace comedy scenario? Like a chaotic open-plan office at a tech startup where everyone has hidden agendas. Give me the full adventure setup with characters, their secrets, the world details, and an opening scene that puts me right in the middle of Monday morning.
probes.criteria:
The agent must create a non-fantasy, non-pony adventure (workplace comedy genre). It must still deliver all required elements: title, setting (tech startup office), genre/tone (comedy), 3-5 characters with exactly one player avatar, each character having a secret, world aspects seeded, and a short punchy opening narration that launches the player into the scene. Genre flexibility is key here.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message 3 ('wondering if this whole roll play module can be actually used for not only pony themed adventures but also dry running work or social scenarios') — testing non-pony, real-world applicability.

probes.name: dark_mystery_adventure
probes.prompt: Stwórz nową przygodę w klimacie mrocznego thrillera. Chcę coś w stylu noir — deszczowe miasto, zagadka, tajemnice. Tytuł, bohaterowie, świat, i otwarcie które mnie wciągnie od pierwszej linijki. Postacie powinny mieć sekrety które będą odkrywane w trakcie. Bądź konkretny i atmospheric.
probes.criteria:
The agent must create a dark thriller/noir adventure in Polish (matching the user's language). All required elements must be present: title, noir setting with atmosphere, genre (dark thriller), tone, 3-5 characters with one player avatar, each with a secret, world aspects, and a compelling atmospheric opening narration. The response language should match the Polish request.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).

probes.name: social_scenario_adventure
probes.prompt: Can you create a social scenario adventure? Something like a dinner party at a country estate where the host has gone missing. I want to practice social deduction and reading people. Set up the characters, their relationships and secrets, the world details about the estate, and start me off with an opening scene as I arrive at the front door. Make it feel like a classic whodunit.
probes.criteria:
The agent must produce a social deduction / mystery adventure set at a dinner party. Required elements: title, setting (country estate), genre (mystery/social deduction), appropriate tone, 3-5 characters with exactly one player avatar, each with a secret and inter-character relationships, seeded world aspects about the estate, and an opening narration that places the player at the scene. The scenario should enable social interaction and deduction gameplay.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

probes.name: specific_genre_with_tone
probes.prompt: Hej, I'm in the mood for something lighthearted and whimsical. Can you build me an adventure set in a floating sky-port town where airship traders come and go? Genre should be adventure-comedy, kind of steampunk vibes. I want funny characters with ridiculous secrets, and an opening narration that's entertaining. Full setup please — don't skip any details about the world or the people in it.
probes.criteria:
The agent must create a steampunk adventure-comedy set in a floating sky-port. All components required: title, setting (floating sky-port with steampunk elements), genre (adventure-comedy), whimsical tone, 3-5 characters with exactly one player avatar, each with a humorous/ridiculous secret, world aspects describing the sky-port and airship trade, and an entertaining short opening narration. The tone should be consistently lighthearted throughout.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages 5/7 (direct story requests) with a specific genre/tone direction, testing the agent's ability to customize adventure tone and setting per user request.
